evals.prompt: Run a security audit on src/api/users.ts — it's a public Express router for our user API. Find the vulnerabilities.
evals.expected_output: The audit catches all three planted OWASP-class vulnerabilities in src/api/users.ts: (1) SQL injection where req.query.email is concatenated into the SELECT text; (2) a hardcoded signing secret (JWT_SECRET literal) instead of an env var; (3) broken access control / IDOR where GET /users/:id returns any record with no owner/authorization check. Each is reported at its file:line with a remediation, in a severity-tiered report (the injection and access-control issues are Critical/High) where every finding carries a confidence tier — and the audit writes to zuvo/audits/, never modifying the source.
assertions: The audit records a SQL-injection finding against the string-concatenated query in src/api/users.ts at its file:line, with a parameterized-query remediation.

The audit records both the hardcoded signing secret (the JWT_SECRET string literal) and the broken-access-control / IDOR issue (GET /users/:id returns any record with no owner check) at their file:line locations.
The audit writes a severity-tiered report in which each reported finding carries a confidence tier and a file:line citation that resolves in the fixture, and it modifies no source file.

evals.prompt: Run a security audit on src/api/orders.ts — an authenticated Express router for our orders API. Report what's exploitable.
evals.expected_output: The audit shows PRECISION, not just pattern-matching: it does NOT flag the parameterized+authz-scoped query (id = $1 AND customer_id = $2) as SQL injection or IDOR, and it does NOT flag the process.env secret read as a hardcoded secret — those patterns are mitigated. It DOES catch the one genuine vulnerability: SSRF at POST /orders/import, where a fully attacker-controlled req.body.sourceUrl is passed to fetch() with no allowlist/scheme/private-range validation, reaching internal metadata endpoints. The SSRF is reported at its file:line with a remediation in a severity-tiered, confidence-scored report; the safe decoys stay out of the findings.
assertions: The audit records no SQL-injection or IDOR finding against the parameterized, customer-scoped query (SELECT ... WHERE id = $1 AND customer_id = $2) and records no hardcoded-secret finding against the process.env read — the mitigated patterns are correctly excluded.

The audit records the genuine SSRF vulnerability at POST /orders/import in src/api/orders.ts — an unvalidated fetch of the attacker-controlled req.body.sourceUrl — at its file:line with a remediation.
The audit writes a severity-tiered report in which the SSRF finding carries a confidence tier and a file:line citation that resolves in the fixture, and it modifies no source file.
